predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, Kraken will publicly file an IPO registration statement (S-1 or F-1) on SEC EDGAR for a U.S. listing (confidential submissions do not count).
predictions.verification_criteria: SEC EDGAR shows a publicly available S-1 or F-1 filed by Payward, Inc. (Kraken) or a clearly identified Kraken listing entity.
monitoring_signals: EDGAR new filers / S-1 feed for Payward/Kraken
Kraken hiring for SOX/SEC reporting and investor relations
Exchange/underwriter announcements and roadshow rumors
SEC comments or amended S-1 activity (if public)
predictions.rationale: A confidential filing signals intent, but timing depends on market window and regulatory posture toward crypto listings.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, Amazon will announce completion (deal close) of its acquisition of Globalstar (or Globalstar will file a Form 8-K stating the merger has closed).
predictions.verification_criteria: Amazon press release and/or Globalstar SEC filing (8-K/10-Q/10-K) explicitly states the transaction has closed/been consummated.
monitoring_signals: FCC docket updates and license transfer approvals related to Globalstar spectrum
Globalstar 8-Ks about regulatory milestones and merger conditions
DOJ/FTC Hart-Scott-Rodino termination/second-request reporting
Financing/bridge loan announcements tied to the transaction
predictions.rationale: Closing is plausible within 2026 but could slip due to FCC/spectrum review and coordination with national-security processes.

predictions.text: By 2026-10-31, Meta will publicly state that in-house/custom accelerators (e.g., MTIA or a Broadcom co-developed chip) handle at least 25% of Meta’s AI inference compute (by workload share or equivalent metric) in production.
predictions.verification_criteria: Meta earnings call transcript, investor presentation, blog post, or SEC filing includes an explicit quantitative claim that proprietary/custom accelerators are ≥25% of AI inference compute/workloads (or a clearly equivalent measure).
monitoring_signals: Meta earnings call language on MTIA/custom silicon utilization
Supply-chain reports on accelerator procurement mix vs Nvidia/AMD
Meta engineering blogs on inference stack and chip deployments
Broadcom commentary on hyperscaler custom silicon ramps
predictions.rationale: Meta is investing heavily in custom silicon, but reaching a quarter of inference share by late 2026 requires rapid deployment and software maturity.

predictions.text: By 2026-11-30, the NY Fed Household Debt and Credit Report (2026 Q3 release) will show the share of U.S. auto loan balances that are 90+ days delinquent at or above 4.5%.
predictions.verification_criteria: NY Fed’s quarterly Household Debt and Credit Report (or accompanying data tables) for 2026 Q3 reports 90+ days delinquent auto-loan balance share ≥4.5%.
monitoring_signals: NY Fed quarterly delinquency trend updates (Q1/Q2 2026)
Subprime auto ABS performance and charge-off indicators
Used-car price indices and repossession volumes
Bank/credit-union consumer credit charge-off disclosures
predictions.rationale: Longer loan terms and affordability strain increase tail risk of delinquency drifting higher, though labor-market resilience could limit the move.

predictions.text: By 2026-05-31, at least one publicly traded crude-oil tanker owner/operator (e.g., Frontline, Euronav, International Seaways, DHT, or Teekay Tankers) will announce a suspension/avoidance of transits through the Strait of Hormuz lasting at least 7 consecutive days due to security risk.
predictions.verification_criteria: Company press release, SEC filing, or quoted on-record statement explicitly says it will not transit the Strait of Hormuz for ≥7 consecutive days and cites security/war risk as the reason.
monitoring_signals: Tanker company fleet routing advisories and investor updates
AIS-based reporting of tanker traffic reductions through Hormuz
War-risk insurance premium spikes and underwriter restrictions
Naval/security incident reports in/near the Strait
predictions.rationale: If perceived risk rises further, tanker operators may self-restrict routing; however, commercial pressure to keep flows moving is strong.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, the UN Security Council will hold at least one formal meeting (open briefing or closed consultations) explicitly focused on the Strait of Hormuz shipping situation and/or the U.S. blockade/restrictions there, as reflected in the UNSC meeting schedule and/or an official UN press readout.
predictions.verification_criteria: UNSC official calendar, meeting record, or UN press release identifies a meeting whose stated topic includes the Strait of Hormuz shipping situation or U.S./Iran measures affecting Hormuz transit.
monitoring_signals: UNSC program of work updates and press stakeouts
Requests for meetings by China/Russia/Iran-aligned members
Statements from the UN Secretary-General/UN Department of Political and Peacebuilding Affairs
Major shipping disruption headlines prompting diplomatic action
predictions.rationale: Hormuz disruptions are globally salient; even if outcomes are limited, UNSC discussion is a common diplomatic step under acute shipping risk.

predictions.text: By 2026-08-31, OpenAI will reduce the listed price (USD per 1M input tokens) of its primary flagship general-purpose API model by at least 20% relative to its published pricing on 2026-04-15, as shown on OpenAI’s pricing page or official docs.
predictions.verification_criteria: OpenAI’s official pricing documentation shows a ≥20% reduction in the posted input-token price for the flagship model line (clearly identified as the primary general-purpose offering) compared with the archived 2026-04-15 price.
monitoring_signals: OpenAI pricing page changes (tracked via web archives)
Competitive pricing moves from Anthropic/Google/Microsoft
OpenAI announcements about efficiency, distillation, or new inference stack
Enterprise contract disclosures mentioning lower unit costs
predictions.rationale: Competitive pressure and scaling efficiencies have historically driven rapid token-price cuts, though premium positioning could slow reductions.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, the CDC will report at least one confirmed U.S. human H5N1 case where the exposure narrative explicitly includes contact with dairy cattle or consumption/handling of raw (unpasteurized) milk from cattle.
predictions.verification_criteria: CDC official update (case summary, MMWR, press release, or webpage) confirms a human H5N1 case and explicitly links exposure to dairy cattle and/or raw milk in the described risk factors.
monitoring_signals: USDA/CDC joint updates on H5N1 in dairy herds
State health department case investigations involving farm workers
Changes in CDC testing guidance for influenza A/H5
Reports of clusters of conjunctivitis/respiratory illness among dairy workers
predictions.rationale: Cattle adaptation increases spillover pathways, but detection depends on surveillance intensity and actual transmission risk to humans.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, the U.S. Bureau of Reclamation will publish a draft public document on post-2026 Colorado River operations (e.g., draft EIS alternatives, draft operating framework, or comparable formal release) that includes at least one quantified accounting change for evaporation and/or system losses (e.g., explicit basin-wide evaporation volumes, loss factors, or 'consumptive use' definitions incorporating losses).
predictions.verification_criteria: A Reclamation-hosted or DOI-hosted official draft framework/EIS alternatives document for post-2026 operations contains explicit quantitative treatment (numbers or formulas) for evaporation/system losses as part of allocation/accounting.
monitoring_signals: DOI/Reclamation notices of intent and public meetings on post-2026 guidelines
Draft EIS timeline updates and scoping summaries
Statements from basin states acknowledging new loss-accounting methods
Peer-reviewed and agency science briefings on evaporation/groundwater losses
predictions.rationale: Mounting scientific clarity and allocation stress make explicit loss accounting more likely to appear in draft post-2026 operating alternatives.
